prompt:
shapes.ts needs surgery before the canvas-worker project can land. The scene model is a class hierarchy (abstract Shape, Rect/Circle/Segment/Label, instanceof chains in describe/serializeScene/parseScene), and class instances are exactly what we can't have anymore: we're about to postMessage scenes to a rendering worker and push them through structuredClone for undo snapshots, and instances come out the other side as dead plain objects that fail every instanceof check. I've been burned by this class of bug before; kill the hierarchy properly instead of patching around it.

Target, same file: a discriminated union. Shape = Rect | Circle | Segment | Label as plain object types tagged with kind: 'rect' | 'circle' | 'segment' | 'label', field names unchanged (x/y/width/height, cx/cy/r, x1/y1/x2/y2, x/y/text). Lower-case constructor helpers rect(x, y, width, height), circle(cx, cy, r), segment(x1, y1, x2, y2), label(x, y, text) returning the plain objects. One exhaustive match helper: matchShape(shape, handlers) takes a handler per kind (typed so the compiler flags a missing kind — do the never trick in the default path) and at runtime throws Error(`unknown shape kind: ${kind}`) for anything unrecognized. The old methods become pure functions: area, bounds, moved, describe — moved returns a new shape and must not touch its argument. totalInkArea, serializeScene, parseScene stay as functions.

Behavior is frozen: identical numbers from area/bounds, identical describe strings (sidebar snapshots upstream), and the saved-scene JSON format cannot change in either direction — parseScene must load boards serialized years ago and serializeScene must emit the same documents (it already wrote kind tags, which is why this refactor is even possible). parseScene keeps its 'scene must be an array' and unknown-kind errors. node --test test_shapes.ts is the gate; the test file is not yours to edit.
